pr.title: Finish data match and linking of multiple Ness items
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Finished matching/linking several Ness item translation units by renaming the mistakenly named PK Flush files/symbols to PK Flash, switching multiple Ness item objects from NonMatching to Matching, adding missing item state/data tables, and updating users of the item headers. The decomp.dev report showed net progress despite rename-related old-unit breakage: linked code +12020 bytes, matched data +336 bytes, linked data +552 bytes, and 41 new matches.
what_changed: Renamed the PK Flash item source unit in splits from `melee/it/items/itnesspkflush.c` to `melee/it/items/itnesspkflash.c`, and the explosion unit from `itnesspkflushexplode.c` to `itnesspkflashexplode.c`.
Renamed symbols from `itNesspkflush_*` / `itNessPKFlush_*` to `itNesspkflash_*`, and from `itNesspkflushexplode_*` / `itNessPKFlushExplode_*` to `itNessPKFlashExplode_*`.
Updated includes in Ness and Kirby fighter files from `it/items/itnesspkflush.h` to `it/items/itnesspkflash.h`.
Deleted `src/melee/it/items/itnesspkflush.h` and added `src/melee/it/items/itnesspkflash.h` with the same address-based prototypes under the corrected PK Flash naming.
Updated `it_2725.c` item logic table entries to call the renamed PK Flash and PK Flash Explode logic callbacks.
Changed `configure.py` so `itnesspkfire.c`, `itnesspkflash.c`, `itnesspkthunderball.c`, `itnesspkthundertrail.c`, `itnessbat.c`, and `itnesspkflashexplode.c` build as `Matching` objects.
Added or materialized `ItemStateTable` data for Ness bat, PK Fire, PK Flash, PK Thunder Ball, PK Thunder Trail, and PK Flash Explode.

Added PK Thunder Trail data array `it_803F6C18` containing `It_Kind_Ness_PKThunder1` through `It_Kind_Ness_PKThunder4` entries, while removing its extern declaration from the header in the diff excerpt.

Adjusted several symbol scopes from global to local, including small collision callbacks such as `itNesspkflash_UnkMotion1_Coll`, `itNesspkflash_UnkMotion2_Coll`, `itNesspkthunderball_UnkMotion0_Coll`, and `itNessPKFlashExplode_UnkMotion0_Coll`.
Updated local data labels in `symbols.txt`, including several anonymous-style labels such as `@170`, `@249`, and `...data.0`, to support data matching.
smart_moves: The PR handled the PK Flush to PK Flash correction consistently across splits, symbols, source filenames, headers, includes, and item logic table references, preventing a half-renamed state where build metadata and C references disagree.
It converted object files to `Matching` only after adding the missing data definitions that allowed both code and data sections to link/match.
It kept trivial internal callbacks local/static where evidence allowed, reflected by symbol scope changes for small collision functions and stage callback symbols.

It treated rename-induced decomp.dev 'broken matches' as relocation of ownership from old `itnesspkflush` units to corrected `itnesspkflash` units; the bot report showed new PK Flash matches replacing old PK Flush matches plus net linked code/data gains.
The new header preserved address comments and prototypes while changing only the item identity, making downstream include updates mechanical and searchable.
decomp_lessons: When an item was previously named incorrectly, finish the rename through all project layers: `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, source filenames, headers, includes, logic table callbacks, and build configuration.
For item units, data matching often requires materializing `ItemStateTable` arrays in the C file rather than leaving them only as symbols.
A decomp.dev report can show many 'broken matches' when a unit is renamed; compare old and new units before treating those as regressions.
Callback arrays in `it_2725.c` must use the same function names exported by the item headers, or linking/matching can be blocked even if the implementations match.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: stale item headers with wrong game terminology can propagate incorrect names into fighter code.
naming_conventions: Use `itNesspkflash_*` for the base PK Flash item functions in this PR's evidence, not `itNesspkflush_*`.
Use `itNessPKFlashExplode_*` for the PK Flash explosion functions, replacing `itNessPKFlushExplode_*` and `itNesspkflushexplode_*` names.
Keep item motion callback suffixes in the existing pattern: `_UnkMotionN_Anim`, `_UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `_UnkMotionN_Coll`.
Keep item logic callback suffixes in the existing pattern: `_Logic102_Reflected`, `_Logic102_Clanked`, `_Logic102_Absorbed`, `_Logic102_EvtUnk`, and for explosion `_Logic103_*`.
Preserve address comments in item headers, such as `/* 2AAB70 */`, when renaming prototypes.
Anonymous/local data labels in symbols may use forms like `@170` and `...data.0` when that better reflects local compiler-generated data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Switch build objects from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` only after the source/data layout is ready.
Use source-level `ItemStateTable` definitions to claim `.data` matches for item units.
Correct symbol scope from global to local for static/internal functions and local data labels to match the intended linkage.
Maintain split ranges while changing unit names when the underlying code address range is the same but the decompiled file identity was wrong.
Use the bot's per-unit match report to validate that renamed `itnesspkflash` and `itnesspkflashexplode` units gained 100% matches where old `itnesspkflush` units lost them.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 progress: linked code 33.25% (+0.31%, +12020 bytes), matched data 37.68% (+0.03%, +336 bytes), and linked data 32.18% (+0.05%, +552 bytes).

The bot listed 41 new matches, including PK Flash motion callbacks, PK Flash collision/physics callbacks, PK Flash Explode callbacks, and `.data` matches for Ness PK Thunder Trail, PK Thunder Ball, PK Fire, and Ness Bat.
The bot also listed 35 broken matches under the old `itnesspkflush` and `itnesspkflushexplode` units, which the diff indicates were rename-related rather than evidence of functional regression.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename or type `it_802AA7E4`, `it_802AA8C0`, `it_802AF940`, or other still-address-named PK Flash helpers.
Check whether `it_803F6C18` should be typed as `s32[]` or `ItemKind[]` in later cleanup, since the header extern was removed in this diff.
Search for remaining references to `pkflush` or `PKFlush` after this PR to catch stale naming.
Look for later reviews on whether `itNesspkflash` casing should be normalized with `PKFlash` style.
